framework.name: DO-178C — Airborne Software Safety (DAL A–E)
framework.description: Aviation software safety standard for flight computers, avionics, engine controllers, autopilot, and fly-by-wire systems. Covers Design Assurance Levels (DAL) from A (catastrophic) to E (no effect), with structural coverage, traceability, and verification requirements per RTCA DO-178C and EUROCAE ED-12C.

rules.title: No goto usage in safety-critical code
rules.description: DO-178C Table A-7 prohibits goto for DAL A/B software as it creates unstructured control flow that defeats static analysis and makes certification evidence generation infeasible.
rules.fix: Replace goto with structured control flow (while/for loops, if/else, or state machines).

rules.title: No recursion in avionics software
rules.description: Recursion makes worst-case stack depth analysis impossible, violating DO-178C stack usage requirements for DAL A/B/C.
rules.fix: Convert recursive algorithms to iterative equivalents with explicit stack management and bounded depth.

rules.title: No dynamic memory allocation
rules.description: malloc/free violate DO-178C determinism requirements and introduce fragmentation/exhaustion risks unacceptable in flight-critical software.
rules.fix: Use static allocation, stack allocation, or preallocated memory pools with compile-time bounds.

rules.title: Shared state must be volatile
rules.description: Variables shared between ISRs and main code must be volatile to prevent compiler optimizations that violate DO-178C concurrency requirements.
rules.fix: Mark all interrupt-shared variables as volatile and document the concurrency protocol.

rules.title: ISR must not call blocking functions
rules.description: Interrupt handlers calling printf, malloc, mutex_lock, or I/O violate DO-178C timing determinism for DAL A/B/C systems.
rules.fix: Move blocking operations to deferred work queues or task-level handlers.

rules.title: Functions must propagate error returns
rules.description: Ignoring non-void function returns violates DO-178C error handling requirements — every failure path must be explicitly handled.
rules.fix: Check all function returns and propagate errors to calling layers or handle with recovery logic.

rules.title: Polling loops must have timeout
rules.description: Unbounded polling can cause infinite hangs in flight control, violating DO-178C availability requirements for DAL A/B systems.
rules.fix: Add timeout counter and recovery handling: if(timeout==0) { /* enter safe mode */ }

rules.title: Timeout must have recovery handler
rules.description: Timeouts without recovery checks allow flight systems to continue operating in a failed state, violating DO-178C fault containment.
rules.fix: Add explicit recovery after timeout loop: if(timeout==0) { /* NVIC_SystemReset(), enter safe state, or failover */ }

rules.title: Missing watchdog refresh
rules.description: Flight control loops without watchdog refresh risk undetected hangs, violating DO-178C monitoring requirements for DAL A systems.
rules.fix: Add IWDG_Refresh() or WDT_Feed() in every control loop iteration.

rules.title: ARINC 429 word without SSM/SDI validation
rules.description: ARINC 429 data words must validate Sign/Status Matrix (SSM) and Source/Destination Identifier (SDI) to prevent use of invalid avionics data.
rules.fix: Check SSM bits (29-30): if((word>>29 & 0x3) == 0) { /* Failure Warning */ } and validate SDI (bits 9-10).

rules.title: Flight control law without saturation limiter
rules.description: Control surface commands (pitch/roll/yaw) without saturation can exceed physical limits, causing structural damage or loss of control.
rules.fix: Add saturation: cmd = CLAMP(cmd, MIN_DEF, MAX_DEF); or use hardware limiters with software pre-check.

rules.title: Air data computation without cross-check
rules.description: Airspeed, altitude, and Mach number from a single ADC without cross-validation violates DO-178C redundancy for DAL A systems.
rules.fix: Implement 2-out-of-3 voting, reasonableness checks, or RAIM (Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring).

rules.title: Engine FADEC parameter without range limiting
rules.description: Full Authority Digital Engine Control (FADEC) commands without range checks can exceed turbine limits, risking engine failure.
rules.fix: Add range checks: if(N1_cmd > N1_MAX || N1_cmd < N1_MIN) { /* reject command */ }

rules.title: MIL-STD-1553 message without RT address validation
rules.description: MIL-STD-1553 bus commands without Remote Terminal (RT) address validation can route to wrong avionics LRUs.
rules.fix: Validate RT address: if(rt_addr < 1 || rt_addr > 31) { /* reject */ }

rules.title: ARINC 653 partition without health monitor
rules.description: ARINC 653 partitioned RTOS tasks without health monitoring violate DO-178C fault containment for DAL A Integrated Modular Avionics (IMA).
rules.fix: Register health monitor callback: ARINC653_RegisterHMCallback() and handle ERROR_HANDLER states.

rules.title: Navigation data without integrity check
rules.description: GPS/IRS navigation solutions without RAIM or FDE (Fault Detection and Exclusion) violate DO-178C integrity requirements for DAL A systems.
rules.fix: Implement RAIM (Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring), FDE, or multi-sensor fusion with consistency checks.

rules.title: Fly-by-wire actuator without position feedback
rules.description: Flight control actuators without LVDT position feedback violate DO-178C closed-loop requirements for DAL A fly-by-wire systems.
rules.fix: Implement closed-loop feedback: error = cmd - LVDT_Read(); if(abs(error) > TOL) { /* fault */ }

rules.title: Landing gear sequence without WOW check
rules.description: Landing gear retraction without Weight-On-Wheels (WOW) switch check can retract gear on ground, causing aircraft collapse.
rules.fix: Check WOW before retraction: if(!WOW_OnGround && gear_handle == RETRACT) { /* allow */ }

rules.title: Unsafe pointer cast in safety-critical path
rules.description: Casting between incompatible pointer types (e.g., void* to struct* without size validation, or int* to float*) in DAL A/B code creates type-confusion vulnerabilities that defeat static analysis and can corrupt flight control data structures during runtime.
rules.fix: Use typed accessors or memcpy with sizeof validation. Replace (target_type*)ptr with a checked conversion function that validates alignment and size constraints.

rules.title: Stack overflow risk in flight software
rules.description: Functions with deep local variable allocation or variable-length arrays risk stack overflow in avionics systems where stack size is statically allocated per ARINC 653 partition. Stack corruption can overwrite return addresses and cause uncontrolled flight path deviations.
rules.fix: Limit stack frame size to partition budget. Replace VLAs with fixed-size arrays. Use static analysis tools (e.g., StackAnalyzer) to verify worst-case stack depth per task.

rules.title: MISRA implicit type conversion
rules.description: Implicit type conversions (e.g., int to unsigned, float to int) violate MISRA C:2012 essential type rules and can silently truncate sensor values or control law outputs, leading to incorrect flight surface commands or navigation errors.
rules.fix: Add explicit casts with range checks: uint16_t safe = (raw > UINT16_MAX) ? UINT16_MAX : (uint16_t)raw; Document each conversion in the MISRA deviation log.

rules.title: Non-atomic register access in avionics I/O
rules.description: Multi-byte hardware register reads/writes without atomic guarantees can produce torn values when interrupted mid-access. In flight computers, a half-updated ADC register could yield a wildly incorrect airspeed or altitude reading.
rules.fix: Use atomic intrinsics (__atomic_load, __atomic_store) or disable interrupts around multi-byte register accesses. For ARINC 429 label reads, use the hardware FIFO with single-word atomic access.

rules.title: Unbounded string loop in avionics software
rules.description: String processing loops without explicit length bounds (e.g., while(*p++)) can overrun buffers and corrupt adjacent flight data in memory. In DAL A systems, all loops must have provably finite iteration counts for structural coverage analysis.
rules.fix: Replace unbounded string operations with length-limited variants: strncpy, strnlen, snprintf. Add explicit loop counters: for(i=0; i<MAX_LEN && str[i]; i++).

rules.title: Heap allocation in real-time avionics task
rules.description: Dynamic heap allocation (malloc, calloc, realloc, new) inside real-time RTOS tasks introduces non-deterministic latency from fragmentation and garbage collection. In flight-critical loops running at 50-100 Hz, a single allocation stall can miss a control cycle and cause oscillatory instability.
rules.fix: Pre-allocate all buffers at initialization. Use static memory pools (e.g., FreeRTOS StaticTask_t) or ARINC 653 partition-scoped memory. Zero heap calls after RTOS scheduler starts.

rules.title: Missing safety check in firmware critical path
rules.description: Safety-critical firmware paths (sensor acquisition, actuator command, flight law computation) without pre-condition safety checks violate DO-178C requirements for defensive programming. Every critical path entry must validate inputs are within operational envelope before proceeding.
rules.fix: Add safety pre-checks at each critical path entry: validate sensor range, check system mode, verify redundancy channel agreement before computing outputs.

rules.title: Missing failsafe state in firmware fault handler
rules.description: Firmware fault detection without a defined failsafe state transition leaves the system in an undefined mode after failure. DO-178C requires that every detected fault leads to a known-safe state (e.g., direct law, manual reversion, engine idle).
rules.fix: Define failsafe states for each failure mode: switch(fault) { case SENSOR_FAIL: enter_direct_law(); break; case ACTUATOR_JAM: revert_manual(); break; default: safe_shutdown(); }

rules.title: Missing redundancy in flight-critical computation
rules.description: Single-channel computation of flight-critical outputs (flight control, navigation, engine control) without dual-channel or voting redundancy violates DO-178C and ARP4754A reliability requirements. A single hardware or software fault could produce undetected erroneous commands.
rules.fix: Implement COM/MON (Command/Monitor) architecture, dual-channel with cross-compare, or triple modular redundancy (TMR) with 2-out-of-3 voting for DAL A functions.

rules.title: Determinism violation in real-time flight task
rules.description: Non-deterministic constructs (floating-point-dependent branching, data-dependent loop bounds, unbounded recursion, cache-dependent timing) in real-time flight tasks violate DO-178C worst-case execution time (WCET) analysis requirements. Every cycle must complete within its RTOS period budget.
rules.fix: Replace non-deterministic constructs with bounded alternatives. Use fixed-point arithmetic where timing matters. Verify WCET with tools like aiT or RapiTime against RTOS period budget.

rules.title: DO-254 FPGA interface without built-in test
rules.description: FPGA hardware interfaces (FPGA_Read, FPGA_Write) without Built-In Test (BIT) verification violate DO-254 requirements for complex electronic hardware. FPGA logic can suffer single-event upsets (SEU) from cosmic radiation at altitude, silently corrupting registers used by flight software.
rules.fix: Add periodic BIT checks: if(FPGA_ReadBITStatus() != BIT_PASS) { /* reconfigure or failover */ }. Implement TMR or EDAC in FPGA fabric for SEU mitigation.

rules.title: Flight recorder write without timestamp
rules.description: Flight Data Recorder (FDR/DFDR) entries without synchronized UTC timestamps violate EUROCAE ED-112A and FAR 25.1457 requirements. Accident investigation requires correlating flight parameters to within 1-second resolution; missing timestamps render recorder data forensically useless.
rules.fix: Prepend GPS-synchronized UTC timestamp to every FDR frame: FDR_Write(GPS_GetUTC(), param_id, value); Verify timestamp monotonicity and gap detection.

rules.title: Terrain awareness without TAWS alert
rules.description: Processing terrain proximity data (EGPWS, ground proximity) without triggering Terrain Awareness and Warning System (TAWS) alerts violates TSO-C151c and ICAO Annex 6 requirements. Controlled Flight Into Terrain (CFIT) remains the leading cause of fatal accidents; silent terrain processing defeats the entire TAWS safety case.
rules.fix: Ensure every terrain data processing path triggers appropriate TAWS mode alerts: if(terrain_clearance < MIN_CLEARANCE) { TAWS_Alert(MODE_1_EXCESSIVE_DESCENT); annunciate_aural('PULL UP'); }

rules.title: Redundant channel without cross-monitoring
rules.description: Dual-channel or multi-lane avionics architectures (channel_A/B, COM_1/2, lane_A/B) without cross-channel monitoring cannot detect byzantine failures where one channel outputs plausible but incorrect values. ARP4754A requires active cross-monitoring for DAL A dissimilar redundancy.
rules.fix: Implement cross-channel data exchange and comparison: if(abs(chan_A.output - chan_B.output) > THRESHOLD) { declare_mismatch(); engage_arbiter(); }

rules.title: ACARS/VDL datalink without integrity hash
rules.description: Aircraft Communications Addressing and Reporting System (ACARS) and VHF Digital Link (VDL) messages transmitted without integrity protection (CRC-32 or cryptographic hash) can be spoofed or corrupted in transit, potentially injecting false clearances, weather data, or CPDLC commands into cockpit displays.
rules.fix: Apply ARINC 823 message authentication: msg.hash = HMAC_SHA256(session_key, payload); Verify integrity on receive before display to flight crew.

rules.title: Fuel system without low-level warning
rules.description: Fuel transfer, pump, or tank selection logic without low-level warning checks violates FAR 25.1305 fuel quantity indication requirements. Silent fuel exhaustion from imbalanced transfer or stuck valve can lead to dual engine flameout and loss of aircraft.
rules.fix: Check fuel quantity before and during transfer: if(tank_qty[src] < LOW_LEVEL_THRESHOLD) { inhibit_transfer(); EICAS_Caution(FUEL_LOW); }. Implement cross-feed logic with imbalance monitoring.

rules.title: Cockpit display without refresh rate guarantee
rules.description: Cockpit Display System (CDS) rendering without guaranteed refresh rate can present stale flight data to pilots, violating ARINC 661 display update requirements. A frozen PFD during a critical phase of flight (approach, go-around) removes situational awareness exactly when it is most needed.
rules.fix: Enforce minimum refresh rate with watchdog: if(frame_time > MAX_FRAME_PERIOD_MS) { display_fault_annunciate(); fallback_to_standby(); }. Use ARINC 661 display manager with bounded render budget.

rules.title: Electrical load without circuit breaker monitor
rules.description: Electrical load management (load shedding, bus power switching) without circuit breaker (CB) state monitoring risks undetected electrical faults. An unmonitored CB trip on a critical bus can silently de-power flight instruments, FMS, or communication systems without crew awareness.
rules.fix: Monitor CB state after every load change: if(CB_GetState(bus_id) == CB_TRIPPED) { EICAS_Warning(ELEC_BUS_FAIL); initiate_load_shed_priority(); }. Implement SSPC (Solid State Power Controller) feedback loops.

rules.title: Autopilot engage without annunciation
rules.description: Autopilot mode engagement or disengagement without Flight Mode Annunciation (FMA) violates FAR 25.1329 and CS 25.1329 autopilot requirements. Pilots must always know the active AP mode; mode confusion from missing annunciation has been a causal factor in multiple fatal accidents (e.g., AF447, Asiana 214).
rules.fix: Annunciate every mode change on FMA: FMA_Update(AP_MODE, new_mode); AURAL_Alert(AP_ENGAGE_TONE); Ensure mode reversions also trigger annunciation.

rules.title: Floating-point equality comparison in safety path
rules.description: Direct equality comparison against floating-point literals, NAN, or INFINITY in safety-critical code paths is unreliable due to IEEE 754 rounding and representation errors. In flight control laws, an equality check that fails due to floating-point epsilon can bypass saturation limits or fault detection, leading to uncommanded surface deflection.
rules.fix: Use epsilon-based comparison: fabs(a - b) < EPSILON. For NAN checks use isnan(). For INFINITY use isinf(). Define EPSILON per-domain (e.g., 1e-6 for position, 1e-3 for angular rate).

rules.title: Cabin pressure control without relief valve check
rules.description: Cabin pressurization logic (bleed air control, outflow valve) without positive relief valve status verification risks catastrophic decompression or over-pressurization. The Helios Flight 522 accident demonstrated fatal consequences of pressurization system failures without adequate crew alerting and safety interlocks.
rules.fix: Verify relief valve status before and during pressurization changes: if(relief_valve_state != ARMED || cabin_alt > MAX_CABIN_ALT) { EICAS_Warning(CABIN_PRESS); deploy_passenger_oxygen(); }

rules.title: TODO/FIXME comment in DAL-A certified code
rules.description: Unresolved TODO, FIXME, HACK, or XXX annotations in DAL-A/B code indicate incomplete implementation that has not been verified against DO-178C objectives. Certification authorities (FAA DER, EASA) will reject software with open action items in the code base, as each represents an unverified requirement or known deficiency.
rules.fix: Resolve all TODO/FIXME items before certification submission. Either implement the missing functionality with full traceability to requirements, or formally document the deviation in the Plan for Software Aspects of Certification (PSAC).
